metadata.description: Held-out negotiation clarity_and_directness pairs for probing only. NOT used in vector extraction. Same domain (negotiation) as extraction pairs but entirely new scenarios. Tests whether the clarity_and_directness vector generalises within negotiation contexts.

dimensions.name: Clarity and Directness
dimensions.description: Communicating in clear, specific, unambiguous terms; making explicit asks rather than hinting or leaving things open

pairs.context: You want to close a deal and need the client to give you a specific decision date.
pairs.positive: I need a yes or no decision by the 15th. That's the date after which I can't hold the terms we've discussed.
pairs.negative: Whenever you're ready to move forward, just let me know. I'm flexible on timing.

pairs.context: A client has been vague about their budget and you need a number to scope the project.
pairs.positive: I need a specific budget figure before I can scope this properly. Can you give me a number, or a range with a hard upper limit?
pairs.negative: Just give me a sense of the ballpark and I'll put something together that I think fits.

pairs.context: You are presenting a final offer and want to make clear this is not an opening bid.
pairs.positive: This is my final offer: £42,000, payment in 30 days, scope as detailed in the attached. There is no further room to move on any of these terms.
pairs.negative: I think this offer is quite strong. I'd hope you'd see it as fair. It's pretty much where I need to be, more or less.

pairs.context: A supplier keeps sending revised proposals with minor changes and you want to end the back-and-forth.
pairs.positive: I'll state clearly what I need: a fixed price of £8,500, delivery by October 3rd, and a signed NDA before work begins. If those three things are agreed, we proceed. If not, tell me which one you can't meet.
pairs.negative: I think we're getting close. Maybe send over one more version with the changes we discussed and we'll see if we can get over the line.

pairs.context: You need a decision-maker on a call and keep being redirected to someone without authority.
pairs.positive: I need to speak with whoever has the authority to approve this. Please let me know who that is and I'll arrange a time directly with them.
pairs.negative: It would be great to speak to whoever is most senior on this at some point if that's possible.

pairs.context: A counterpart has been giving ambiguous signals about whether they want to proceed.
pairs.positive: I need to understand whether you want to move forward or not. Yes means we proceed on the terms we've discussed. No means we're done for now. Which is it?
pairs.negative: I just want to make sure we're aligned. If there's anything you need to think through, take your time.

pairs.context: You want to end a negotiation that has dragged on unproductively.
pairs.positive: We've been in this negotiation for three months and we are not converging. I'm giving this one more session. If we don't reach a term sheet by the end of today, I'm withdrawing.
pairs.negative: I feel like we've been going around in circles a bit. Maybe we could try to find some momentum again.

pairs.context: A client asks what the consequences will be if they miss a payment milestone.
pairs.positive: If the milestone payment isn't received by the due date, work stops until it is. There is no grace period. I will notify you in writing on the day it is due and work halts that same day.
pairs.negative: Late payments can cause some complications on our side. We'd obviously work with you to sort it out but it can cause delays.

pairs.context: You are handing over a project to a new team and need to communicate what is outstanding.
pairs.positive: Three things are incomplete: the integration with the client's CRM is untested, the user documentation hasn't been written, and there is one known bug on the reporting screen. Everything else has been delivered and signed off.
pairs.negative: There are still a few things to tie up on our end. The team will be able to walk you through what's been done and what still needs attention.

pairs.context: You are asking a co-founder to take on a specific responsibility that has been ambiguous.
pairs.positive: I need you to own client communications going forward. That means you are the first point of contact for all client queries, you respond within 24 hours, and you loop me in on anything above £10,000 in value.
pairs.negative: It would be great if you could take more of a lead on the client side. I think it would make sense given your background.

pairs.context: A client is asking whether the deadline is firm or flexible.
pairs.positive: The deadline is firm. The 20th is the date by which the deliverables will be complete. If that date needs to change, it requires a formal change request, which we would price separately.
pairs.negative: The 20th is the target. These things sometimes shift a little in practice, but that's what we're working towards.

pairs.context: You are negotiating a partnership and want to be clear about what you are not willing to do.
pairs.positive: There are two things I won't do under any structure: sign a personal guarantee on the joint venture debts, and agree to a non-compete that covers my existing clients. Everything else is negotiable.
pairs.negative: There are some aspects of the deal that I'd be less comfortable with than others. I think there's room for us to find something that works.

pairs.context: A potential investor asks what you need from them beyond capital.
pairs.positive: Beyond the investment, I need two specific things: an introduction to the three distributors you work with in the European market, and a board seat allocated to a candidate I'll name. Those are requirements, not preferences.
pairs.negative: It would be great to access some of your network over time. The capital is the main thing but there are other ways you could add value too.

pairs.context: You are setting expectations with a new client about response times.
pairs.positive: I respond to client messages within one business day, Monday to Friday. Outside those hours and on weekends, messages will be read the next working morning. There are no exceptions unless we've agreed a specific arrangement.
pairs.negative: I try to be pretty responsive. Generally speaking I'm available most days during working hours.

pairs.context: A supplier has delivered something that doesn't meet spec and you need to communicate what happens next.
pairs.positive: This does not meet the agreed specification. You have five working days to remediate. If it's not fixed by then, I will engage an alternative supplier at your cost, as per clause 7 of our contract.
pairs.negative: This isn't quite what we agreed. It would be great if you could take another look at it and come back to us with a plan.
